content.full_text:
Law Offices of OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH

March 13, 1992

Mr. Wayne Imbieri  
Technical Assistance & Analysis Division  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street  
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Wayne:

This letter is sent to confirm telephone advice you provided to me concerning the 1992 campaign reporting obligations for a state senator who is not on the ballot in 1992.

Government Code section 84200.5(a), as amended effective January 1, 1992, provides, in relevant part, that "all elected state officers who, during the reporting periods covered by Section 84200.7, contribute to any committee required to report receipts, expenditures, or contributions pursuant to this title, or make an independent expenditure, shall file the preelection statements specified in Section 84200.7."

I asked you whether a first preelection report would be required if, during the reporting period, the state senator's controlled committee had made no contributions to other candidates or committees and no independent expenditures but had made transfers to other campaign committees controlled by the state senator. You stated that a transfer would not constitute a "contribution" within the meaning of section 84200.5 and therefore a first preelection report would not be required under these circumstances.

Please call me right away if I have stated your advice incorrectly. Thank you for your time and attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH

[signature]
DIANE M. FISHBURN
DMF/kjh
